serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 10 hand-breaded cauliflower bites, weighing approximately 140 g, typically served with a dipping sauce such as ranch, BBQ, or honey mustard.

nutrient_source.title: Cauliflower, cooked, breaded, fried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for Shake Shack Cauliflower Bites. Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for breaded fried cauliflower (NDB 11136 and related entries for breaded fried vegetables) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for comparable breaded fried cauliflower products. Serving size of 140 g estimated from Shake Shack menu description of 10-count portion. Per-100g values derived from this composite estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of breaded fried vegetables — analogous estimate based on battered/breaded cauliflower and comparable fried vegetable products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Shake Shack Cauliflower Bites. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for breaded and battered fried vegetable products and wheat-flour-based coatings. The wheat-flour breadcrumb coating is the primary glycemic driver; cauliflower itself has a low GI (~15), but the breading raises the composite GI to the low range. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 34.3) / 100 ≈ 18.9, where carbohydrate per 140 g serving = 24.5 × 1.4 = 34.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~532 mg per 10-count serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size, as one 10-count serving contributes approximately 532 mg sodium (~23% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), and the dipping sauces (ranch, BBQ, honey mustard) add further sodium that should be accounted for.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~55) but moderate glycemic load (~18.9 per serving) driven by wheat-flour breadcrumb coating
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume cauliflower bites in moderation; while the glycemic index is low (55), the breaded wheat-flour coating results in a moderate glycemic load of approximately 18.9 per 10-count serving, which can still affect postprandial blood glucose, and accompanying dipping sauces may add additional sugars.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat (13 g/100 g) with saturated fat (1.8 g/100 g) and trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g) from deep-frying
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should be aware that deep-frying adds total and saturated fat; while saturated fat per serving is relatively modest (~2.5 g), the sodium load and potential trans fat from frying oil warrant occasional rather than regular consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cauliflower Bites (10 Count) is a fast-food snack item served at Shake Shack restaurants in Singapore, consisting of ten hand-breaded crispy cauliflower florets that are deep-fried to a golden, crunchy exterior. The cauliflower is coated in a seasoned wheat-flour breadcrumb batter before frying, yielding a tender interior and a crisp, savoury crust. The dish is served with a choice of dipping sauce — ranch, BBQ, or honey mustard — which adds additional flavour, sodium, and calories beyond the bites themselves. Nutritionally, per 100 g the bites provide approximately 230 kcal, 4.5 g protein, 13 g fat, and 24.5 g carbohydrate, with the breadcrumb coating being the primary source of carbohydrates and the frying oil contributing the majority of fat. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), driven by the wheat-flour coating rather than the cauliflower itself. The sodium content (~380 mg/100 g) is clinically relevant for individuals managing hypertension. The dish is vegetarian but not vegan due to the egg-containing batter, and it contains gluten allergens. It is positioned as a shareable appetiser or side dish in the American fast-casual dining context, and is available at Shake Shack outlets in Singapore including 89 Neil Road and One Fullerton.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Shake Shack cauliflower bites?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data exists for Shake Shack Singapore Cauliflower Bites in any approved primary source (HPB Foodo, USDA Branded Food, or ICMR-NIN). Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for comparable breaded fried cauliflower products and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for similar fast-food breaded vegetable items. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for breaded/battered fried vegetable products. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on USDA FoodData Central data for breaded fried cauliflower; no exact Shake Shack Singapore nutritional panel was available in any approved source at time of processing. 2. Serving size of 140 g is estimated from the 10-count portion description; actual weight may vary by restaurant preparation. 3. Dipping sauces (ranch, BBQ, honey mustard) are not included in the per-100g nutrient values and would add additional calories, fat, sodium, and sugar. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as cauliflower and plant-based ingredients contain no vitamin B12; this is naturally zero for a plant-derived food with no animal-derived ingredients in the base bites (egg in batter contributes negligible B12 at this level). 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central reference data for this food category. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central reference data for breaded fried cauliflower. 7. trans_fat_g of 0.1 g/100g reflects trace trans fats potentially present from commercial frying oils; this is a conservative estimate. 8. The food_group in the input JSON was listed as 'Meat and alternatives', which is incorrect for a vegetable-based snack; corrected to 'Snacks' in this record. 9. Cuisine corrected from 'Burgers' (a field survey category tag) to 'American / Western Fast Food' reflecting the actual cuisine type. 10. GI analogous estimate basis: University of Sydney GI Database data for wheat-flour-battered fried vegetable products; cauliflower base GI ~15 (low), composite with breading estimated at 55 (low GI category). 11. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore.
